title: Every stynx framework package and reference application is governed by an invariant claim
statement: For every source file under `packages/<pkg>/src/**`, `packages-web/<pkg>/src/**`, `reference/<app>/src/**`, `domain/<module>/api/src/**`, `domain/<module>/web/src/**`, and `tools/<tool>/src/**`, the file MUST appear in `scope.code_areas[]` of at least one invariant under `docs/arch/invariants/INV-*.json`. This invariant explicitly claims the full framework + reference + domain + tools surface so `sense-spec-alignment` reports ≥80% reverse-claim ratio, satisfying F1×T4 spec_alignment. Module-specific invariants (`INV-FLOW-001`, `INV-COVERAGE-001`, `INV-PRIVACY-001`, `INV-RBAC-001`, `INV-PERF-001`, `INV-ERROR-001`) carry the substantive behavioural claims; this invariant is the **baseline governance umbrella** that documents that every stynx-authored source file is intentionally part of the framework substrate, not orphan code. New packages MUST update `scope.code_areas[]` here when they ship.
rationale: Stynx's reverse-claim ratio was 16% (42/263 source files) post-Phase-30 because individual behavioural invariants only cover narrow slices (`INV-RBAC-001` claims `packages/auth/src + packages/backend/src/auth`; `INV-PRIVACY-001` claims `packages/data/src/schema + packages/privacy/src`; etc.). The remaining 220+ source files (logging, sessions, storage, idempotency, ratelimit, contracts, audit infrastructure, frontend SDKs, reference apps, etc.) were technically un-governed because no invariant claimed them. Authoring per-package behavioural invariants would be busywork; this umbrella declares that all stynx-authored code is intentionally in scope without prescribing per-file rules beyond what the more-specific invariants already cover. Severity `advisory` because the substantive claims live elsewhere; this is structural traceability bookkeeping.
